problem.summary: Compute a numerically stable softmax for each row of a row-major matrix. The baseline assigns one thread to each row. The optimized version assigns one block to each row and uses shared-memory reductions for max and sum.
outputs: Each output row is softmax(input row) and sums approximately to 1
constraints: The optimized seed assumes moderate column counts that fit one block-per-row reduction

verification.method: Compare against CPU stable softmax and check row sums. The 4096x1024 and 4096x1000 gfx1201 HIP runs passed with max_abs_error=0 and max_row_sum_error<=1e-6.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. On 2026-05-28, AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT gfx1201 HIP-event timing measured 0.57060 ms baseline versus 0.07170 ms optimized for 4096x1024 fp32 softmax, speedup 7.958159x; and 0.34105 ms baseline versus 0.07160 ms optimized for 4096x1000 fp32 softmax, speedup 4.763268x. A PyTorch eager ROCm timing-only baseline is also attached. No rocprofiler/rocprof counters were collected.
